speaker: SUFFOLK
text: As by your high imperial Majesty

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I had in charge at my depart for France,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: As procurator to your Excellence,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To marry Princess Margaret for your Grace,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: So, in the famous ancient city Tours,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: In presence of the Kings of France and Sicil,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: The Dukes of Orleance, Calaber, Britaigne, and Alanson,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Seven earls, twelve barons, and twenty reverend bishops,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I have performed my task and was espoused;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And humbly now upon my bended knee,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: In sight of England and her lordly peers,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Deliver up my title in the Queen

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To your most gracious hands, that are the substance

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Of that great shadow I did represent:

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: The happiest gift that ever marquess gave,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: The fairest queen that ever king received.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Suffolk, arise.—Welcome, Queen Margaret.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I can express no kinder sign of love

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Than this kind kiss.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O Lord, that lends me life,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Lend me a heart replete with thankfulness!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For Thou hast given me in this beauteous face

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: A world of earthly blessings to my soul,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If sympathy of love unite our thoughts.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Great king of England and my gracious lord,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The mutual conference that my mind hath had

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: By day, by night, waking and in my dreams,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: In courtly company or at my beads,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: With you, mine alderliefest sovereign,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Makes me the bolder to salute my king

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: With ruder terms, such as my wit affords

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And overjoy of heart doth minister.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Her sight did ravish, but her grace in speech,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Her words yclad with wisdom’s majesty,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Makes me from wond’ring fall to weeping joys,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Such is the fullness of my heart’s content.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Lords, with one cheerful voice welcome my love.

speaker: ALL
text: Long live Queen Margaret, England’s happiness!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: We thank you all.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: My Lord Protector, so it please your Grace,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Here are the articles of contracted peace

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Between our sovereign and the French king Charles,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: For eighteen months concluded by consent.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Imprimis, it is agreed between the

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: French king Charles and William de la Pole, Marquess

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: of Suffolk, ambassador for Henry, King of England,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: that the said Henry shall espouse the Lady

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Margaret, daughter unto Reignier, King of Naples,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Sicilia, and Jerusalem, and crown her Queen of England

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: ere the thirtieth of May next ensuing. Item,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: that the duchy of Anjou and the county of Maine

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: shall be released and delivered to the King her

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: father—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Uncle, how now?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Pardon me, gracious lord.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Some sudden qualm hath struck me at the heart

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And dimmed mine eyes, that I can read no further.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Uncle of Winchester, I pray read on.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Item, it is further

speaker: CARDINAL
text: agreed between them that the duchies of

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Anjou and Maine shall be released and delivered to

speaker: CARDINAL
text: the King her father, and she sent over of the King of

speaker: CARDINAL
text: England’s own proper cost and charges, without

speaker: CARDINAL
text: having any dowry.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: They please us well.—Lord Marquess, kneel down.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We here create thee the first Duke of Suffolk

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text:
And girt thee with the sword.
                 
                Suffolk
                 
                rises
                .
               Cousin of York,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We here discharge your Grace from being regent

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I’ th’ parts of France till term of eighteen months

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Be full expired.—Thanks, Uncle Winchester,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Gloucester, York, Buckingham, Somerset,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Salisbury, and Warwick;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We thank you all for this great favor done

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: In entertainment to my princely queen.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Come, let us in, and with all speed provide

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To see her coronation be performed.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Brave peers of England, pillars of the state,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To you Duke Humphrey must unload his grief,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Your grief, the common grief of all the land.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: What, did my brother Henry spend his youth,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: His valor, coin, and people in the wars?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Did he so often lodge in open field,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: In winter’s cold and summer’s parching heat,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To conquer France, his true inheritance?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And did my brother Bedford toil his wits

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To keep by policy what Henry got?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Have you yourselves, Somerset, Buckingham,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Brave York, Salisbury, and victorious Warwick,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Received deep scars in France and Normandy?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Or hath mine uncle Beaufort and myself,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: With all the learnèd council of the realm,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Studied so long, sat in the Council House,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Early and late, debating to and fro

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: How France and Frenchmen might be kept in awe,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And had his Highness in his infancy

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Crowned in Paris in despite of foes?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And shall these labors and these honors die?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Shall Henry’s conquest, Bedford’s vigilance,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Your deeds of war, and all our counsel die?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: O peers of England, shameful is this league,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Fatal this marriage, cancelling your fame,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Blotting your names from books of memory,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Razing the characters of your renown,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Defacing monuments of conquered France,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Undoing all, as all had never been!

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Nephew, what means this passionate discourse,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: This peroration with such circumstance?

speaker: CARDINAL
text: For France, ’tis ours, and we will keep it still.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Ay, uncle, we will keep it if we can,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: But now it is impossible we should.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Suffolk, the new-made duke that rules the roast,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Hath given the duchy of Anjou and Maine

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Unto the poor King Reignier, whose large style

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Agrees not with the leanness of his purse.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Now, by the death of Him that died for all,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: These counties were the keys of Normandy.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: But wherefore weeps Warwick, my valiant son?

speaker: WARWICK
text: For grief that they are past recovery;

speaker: WARWICK
text: For, were there hope to conquer them again,

speaker: WARWICK
text: My sword should shed hot blood, mine eyes no tears.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Anjou and Maine? Myself did win them both!

speaker: WARWICK
text: Those provinces these arms of mine did conquer.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And are the cities that I got with wounds

speaker: WARWICK
text: Delivered up again with peaceful words?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Mort Dieu!

speaker: YORK
text: For Suffolk’s duke, may he be suffocate

speaker: YORK
text: That dims the honor of this warlike isle!

speaker: YORK
text: France should have torn and rent my very heart

speaker: YORK
text: Before I would have yielded to this league.

speaker: YORK
text: I never read but England’s kings have had

speaker: YORK
text: Large sums of gold and dowries with their wives;

speaker: YORK
text: And our King Henry gives away his own

speaker: YORK
text: To match with her that brings no vantages.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: A proper jest, and never heard before,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: That Suffolk should demand a whole fifteenth

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: For costs and charges in transporting her!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: She should have stayed in France and starved in France

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Before—

speaker: CARDINAL
text: My lord of Gloucester, now you grow too hot.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: It was the pleasure of my lord the King.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: My lord of Winchester, I know your mind.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: ’Tis not my speeches that you do mislike,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: But ’tis my presence that doth trouble you.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Rancor will out. Proud prelate, in thy face

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I see thy fury. If I longer stay,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: We shall begin our ancient bickerings.—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Lordings, farewell; and say, when I am gone,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I prophesied France will be lost ere long.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: So, there goes our Protector in a rage.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: ’Tis known to you he is mine enemy,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Nay, more, an enemy unto you all,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: And no great friend, I fear me, to the King.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Consider, lords, he is the next of blood

speaker: CARDINAL
text: And heir apparent to the English crown.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Had Henry got an empire by his marriage,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: And all the wealthy kingdoms of the West,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: There’s reason he should be displeased at it.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Look to it, lords. Let not his smoothing words

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Bewitch your hearts; be wise and circumspect.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: What though the common people favor him,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Calling him “Humphrey, the good Duke of Gloucester,”

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Clapping their hands and crying with loud voice

speaker: CARDINAL
text: “Jesu maintain your royal Excellence!”

speaker: CARDINAL
text: With “God preserve the good Duke Humphrey!”

speaker: CARDINAL
text: I fear me, lords, for all this flattering gloss,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: He will be found a dangerous Protector.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Why should he, then, protect our sovereign,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: He being of age to govern of himself?—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Cousin of Somerset, join you with me,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And all together, with the Duke of Suffolk,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: We’ll quickly hoise Duke Humphrey from his seat.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: This weighty business will not brook delay.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: I’ll to the Duke of Suffolk presently.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Cousin of Buckingham, though Humphrey’s pride

speaker: SOMERSET
text: And greatness of his place be grief to us,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Yet let us watch the haughty cardinal.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: His insolence is more intolerable

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Than all the princes’ in the land besides.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: If Gloucester be displaced, he’ll be Protector.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Or thou or I, Somerset, will be Protector,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Despite Duke Humphrey or the Cardinal.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Pride went before; Ambition follows him.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: While these do labor for their own preferment,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Behooves it us to labor for the realm.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: I never saw but Humphrey, Duke of Gloucester,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Did bear him like a noble gentleman.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Oft have I seen the haughty cardinal,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: More like a soldier than a man o’ th’ Church,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: As stout and proud as he were lord of all,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Swear like a ruffian and demean himself

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Unlike the ruler of a commonweal.—

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Warwick, my son, the comfort of my age,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Thy deeds, thy plainness, and thy housekeeping

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Hath won the greatest favor of the Commons,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Excepting none but good Duke Humphrey.—

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And, brother York, thy acts in Ireland,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: In bringing them to civil discipline,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Thy late exploits done in the heart of France,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: When thou wert regent for our sovereign,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Have made thee feared and honored of the people.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Join we together for the public good

speaker: SALISBURY
text: In what we can to bridle and suppress

speaker: SALISBURY
text: The pride of Suffolk and the Cardinal,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: With Somerset’s and Buckingham’s ambition;

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And, as we may, cherish Duke Humphrey’s deeds

speaker: SALISBURY
text: While they do tend the profit of the land.

speaker: WARWICK
text: So God help Warwick, as he loves the land

speaker: WARWICK
text: And common profit of his country!

speaker: YORK
text:
And so says York—
                aside
                 
              for he hath greatest cause.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Then let’s make haste away and look unto the main.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Unto the main? O father, Maine is lost!

speaker: WARWICK
text: That Maine which by main force Warwick did win

speaker: WARWICK
text: And would have kept so long as breath did last!

speaker: WARWICK
text: Main chance, father, you meant; but I meant Maine,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Which I will win from France or else be slain.

speaker: YORK
text: Anjou and Maine are given to the French;

speaker: YORK
text: Paris is lost; the state of Normandy

speaker: YORK
text: Stands on a tickle point now they are gone.

speaker: YORK
text: Suffolk concluded on the articles,

speaker: YORK
text: The peers agreed, and Henry was well pleased

speaker: YORK
text: To change two dukedoms for a duke’s fair daughter.

speaker: YORK
text: I cannot blame them all. What is ’t to them?

speaker: YORK
text: ’Tis thine they give away, and not their own.

speaker: YORK
text: Pirates may make cheap pennyworths of their pillage,

speaker: YORK
text: And purchase friends, and give to courtesans,

speaker: YORK
text: Still reveling like lords till all be gone;

speaker: YORK
text: Whileas the silly owner of the goods

speaker: YORK
text: Weeps over them, and wrings his hapless hands,

speaker: YORK
text: And shakes his head, and trembling stands aloof,

speaker: YORK
text: While all is shared and all is borne away,

speaker: YORK
text: Ready to starve, and dare not touch his own.

speaker: YORK
text: So York must sit and fret and bite his tongue

speaker: YORK
text: While his own lands are bargained for and sold.

speaker: YORK
text: Methinks the realms of England, France, and Ireland

speaker: YORK
text: Bear that proportion to my flesh and blood

speaker: YORK
text: As did the fatal brand Althaea burnt

speaker: YORK
text: Unto the Prince’s heart of Calydon.

speaker: YORK
text: Anjou and Maine both given unto the French!

speaker: YORK
text: Cold news for me, for I had hope of France,

speaker: YORK
text: Even as I have of fertile England’s soil.

speaker: YORK
text: A day will come when York shall claim his own;

speaker: YORK
text: And therefore I will take the Nevilles’ parts

speaker: YORK
text: And make a show of love to proud Duke Humphrey,

speaker: YORK
text: And, when I spy advantage, claim the crown,

speaker: YORK
text: For that’s the golden mark I seek to hit.

speaker: YORK
text: Nor shall proud Lancaster usurp my right,

speaker: YORK
text: Nor hold the scepter in his childish fist,

speaker: YORK
text: Nor wear the diadem upon his head,

speaker: YORK
text: Whose churchlike humors fits not for a crown.

speaker: YORK
text: Then, York, be still awhile till time do serve.

speaker: YORK
text: Watch thou and wake, when others be asleep,

speaker: YORK
text: To pry into the secrets of the state

speaker: YORK
text: Till Henry, surfeiting in joys of love

speaker: YORK
text: With his new bride and England’s dear-bought queen,

speaker: YORK
text: And Humphrey with the peers be fall’n at jars.

speaker: YORK
text: Then will I raise aloft the milk-white rose,

speaker: YORK
text: With whose sweet smell the air shall be perfumed,

speaker: YORK
text: And in my standard bear the arms of York,

speaker: YORK
text: To grapple with the house of Lancaster;

speaker: YORK
text: And force perforce I’ll make him yield the crown,

speaker: YORK
text: Whose bookish rule hath pulled fair England down.
